title: Replace the XGBoost ranker with CatBoost group ranking on race-lap choice sets
summary: The current rank experiment uses XGBoost `rank:pairwise`, but CatBoost has richer pairwise/groupwise objectives and handles raw high-cardinality categories without manual coding.
rationale: The current rank experiment uses XGBoost `rank:pairwise`, but CatBoost has richer pairwise/groupwise objectives and handles raw high-cardinality categories without manual coding. In this task, each race-lap is a natural query group where the model ranks which drivers are most likely to pit next.
implementation_hint: Set `GroupId = Year_Race_LapNumber` and train CatBoost with `YetiRankPairwise`, `PairLogitPairwise`, or `QuerySoftMax` on raw `Driver/Race/Compound` plus causal history features. Use OOF rank-normalized predictions and blend them with the standard classifier instead of raw probability averaging.
expected_effect: Can outperform the current XGB ranker by using better categorical interactions inside within-lap ranking, especially for rare drivers and compounds.
risk: Pairwise objectives are slower and may optimize query ordering better than absolute probability; benefit may appear mostly in the ensemble.
